title: Candidate dimension row (canonical, per-contest)
description: Logical contract for one row of datasets/elections/dim_candidates.parquet. One row per (ac_id, period_label, ballot_serial). Holds the denormalised strings (candidate name, party_id) that elections/election_results.parquet does not carry. PK = candidate_id, which matches the per-contest entity_id constructed by canonical/adapters/eci/identity.candidate_entity_id. Read by frontend view-model loaders that JOIN election_results + dim_candidates to reconstruct citizen-facing shapes (per TODO/20260517-canonical-long-format-pivot.md sub-phase 1.2b). NOT modelled as observations because name/party are per-entity attributes, not time-varying numeric facts (OWID fact/dim separation).

x-changelog.description: Initial dim_candidates schema (Phase 1.2b). Columns: candidate_id PK, ac_id, period_label, ballot_serial, name, party_id, rank, source_id.

Additive (PR-R.2 / no-UNK-regression). Added optional nullable column `party_short_raw` carrying the verbatim party_short string from the upstream ECI candidate row. Lets the citizen UI fall back to the actual upstream abbreviation (e.g. 'BSP', 'NTK', 'TVK', 'SDPI') when party_lookup couldn't resolve the row and party_id collapsed to 'parties.IN.UNK'. Prevents the regression where the canonical view-model surfaces a literal 'UNK' chip for ~1.5k long-tail candidates spread across ~380 fringe parties. Nullable for NOTA (which carries no upstream party label) and for any future row that lacks an upstream short_name string. Backwards-compatible: existing readers that only project the v1.0 columns are unaffected.

Additive (PR-S.1 / biographic lift onto dim_candidates — canonical pivot row 1.8f). Added six optional nullable columns lifting the citizen-readable biographic fields ECI publishes in Statistical Report PDFs: `sex` (Male/Female/Other/null), `age` (integer 18-120/null), `education` (11-value enum verbatim from ECI codebook + null), `profession` (17-value enum verbatim from ECI codebook + null), `constituency_type` (GEN/SC/ST/null seat reservation), `party_type` (ECI 5-bucket party classification NATIONAL/STATE/OTHER_STATE/REGISTERED_UNRECOGNISED/INDEPENDENT/null). Enums copied verbatim from people.entity.schema.json v1.0 — single source of truth migrates from per-candidate JSON sidecars under datasets/people/<event>/<ac>/<slug>.json to inline columns on dim_candidates.parquet. PR-S.2 retires the JSON sidecars and the paired schema. Backwards-compatible: existing readers that only project the v1.1 columns are unaffected; pre-existing rows get NULL for the six new columns via DuckDB's INSERT BY NAME merge in writer._upsert_dim. Provenance grade per field (issuing_authority vs sworn_declaration) is NOT carried inline — sworn_declaration is the publisher-level grade for the ECI Statistical Report adapter and is recorded on the source_id row in taxonomy/sources.parquet.

candidate_id.description: PK. Per-contest candidate entity_id per canonical-store.md §3a (e.g. 'IN-S22-AC-2008-167-AcGenApr2021-C01'). Matches observations.entity_id for candidate-* rows.

period_label.description: ECI event id (e.g. 'AcGenApr2021'). Together with ac_id + ballot_serial uniquely identifies the candidate.

ballot_serial.description: Position on the ECI ballot. Phase 1.1 uses candidate rank as a stand-in until Section-10 surfaces the actual serial (see observations.py:54).

name.description: Candidate display name as printed by ECI. Nullable for NOTA rows or where the upstream omitted it. Citizens see this verbatim.

party_id.description: FK -> dim_parties.party_id. Resolved via canonical/adapters/eci/party_lookup; falls back to 'parties.IN.IND' for independents and 'parties.IN.UNK' for unresolved entries.

rank.description: Final vote-rank within this contest. 1 = winner. Mirrors observations.candidate-rank.

source_id.description: FK -> taxonomy/sources.parquet. Same provenance row as the candidate's observations.

party_short_raw.description: v1.1 (additive). Verbatim ECI party_short string from the upstream candidate row (e.g. 'BSP', 'NTK', 'SDPI'). Citizen-facing UI uses this as the display fallback when party_id == 'parties.IN.UNK' so the chip never shows the literal sentinel. Nullable for NOTA rows and for any row where the upstream omits the short string. NOT a substitute for party_id -- party_id remains the join key into dim_parties.

sex.description: v1.2 (additive, PR-S.1). Normalised from ECI codebook values. Optional; null when not declared. Enum copied verbatim from people.entity.schema.json v1.0.

age.description: v1.2 (additive, PR-S.1). Candidate age at nomination. Lower bound 18 = constitutional minimum (Art. 173(b)). Null when not declared.

education.description: v1.2 (additive, PR-S.1). Indian credential ladder. Self-declared on Form-26 affidavit; no authority verifies. Optional; null when blank. Enum copied verbatim from people.entity.schema.json v1.0.

profession.description: v1.2 (additive, PR-S.1). Self-declared profession category. 17 verbatim values + null when blank. Class distinctions (Agriculture vs Agricultural Labour) preserved deliberately. Enum copied verbatim from people.entity.schema.json v1.0.

constituency_type.description: v1.2 (additive, PR-S.1). Seat reservation category. Null when not declared.

party_type.description: v1.2 (additive, PR-S.1). ECI's 5-bucket party classification (ECI codebook). Null when not declared.
